intro: Syringa albiflora, commonly known as the white lilac or Chinese white lilac, is a deciduous shrub native to China, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Syringa albiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Syringa albiflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
